title: Storage Dining Table
summary: A round-top pedestal-style dining table with two open storage shelves built into the base. Uses a store-bought round top and bun feet.
description:
This is a small round dining table with a genuinely useful trick: the base isn't a simple pedestal, it's a two-shelf storage cube. The four legs frame two open 20" x 20" shelves — a spot for placemats, board games, a fruit bowl — right under the table where you'd otherwise have wasted space.

The build leans on store-bought parts to skip the hard stuff. The round top is a pre-made unfinished 42"–48" tabletop from a home center, and the feet are pre-cut bun feet, so you're not turning a pedestal or gluing up a circle. What you build is the square base: legs made of paired 1x2 and 1x3, two plywood shelves, and a double apron (an inner apron for structure and an outer 1x3 apron for looks) that ties it all together and gives the top something to bolt to.

The legs are L-shaped in section — a 1x2 and a 1x3 nailed together — which is what lets the aprons and shelves land cleanly on two faces of each corner. Watch the two apron layers: the inner apron carries the load and receives the tabletop screws; the outer apron is the finished face. When you attach the top, predrill through the inner apron and be careful not to drill through the top itself. If your bun feet came with hanger bolts, use the surface-mount plates; if not, just screw up through the bottom shelf into the feet with a long predrilled screw.

tools.note: Predrill the tabletop and foot screws to avoid splitting.

materials.name: Hardwood plywood or MDF, 3/4 in (~1/4 sheet)
1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Leg parts, inner apron, shelf trim.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four leg 1x2 parts at 28-1/2", two inner apron (1x2 sides) at 20", two inner apron (1x3 sides) at 18-1/2", four shelf trim (1x2 sides) at 17" and four shelf trim (1x3 sides) at 16-1/2" from the 1x2. Cut four leg 1x3 parts at 28-1/2", two outer apron (1x2 sides) at 17" and two outer apron (1x3 sides) at 16-1/2" from the 1x3. Cut two shelves at 20" from the 3/4 in plywood. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft 1x2 and two 8 ft 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the legs
steps.body: Build each leg by nailing a 1x2 and a 1x3 together into an L-shaped section, using glue. The L profile gives you two clean faces at each corner for the aprons and shelves to land on. Make all four identical.

steps.title: Attach the shelves to the legs
steps.body: Fasten the two plywood shelves to the legs, nailing through the outsides of the legs into the edges of the shelves. The shelves set the base's square footprint, so check they sit level and the legs stay parallel.
materials: Hardwood plywood or MDF, 3/4 in (~1/4 sheet)

steps.title: Add the inner apron
steps.body: Attach the inner apron pieces to the 1x2 faces of the legs with 1-1/4" nails, and nail through the 1x3 legs into the end edges of the apron. Repeat around all four sides. This inner apron is the structural ring and the layer the tabletop will screw into later.

steps.title: Add the outer apron and shelf trim
steps.body: Add the outer 1x3 apron pieces as the finished face, keeping top edges flush. Then trim the shelf edges with the 1x2 shelf-trim pieces. These layers hide the plywood edges and give the base a solid, furniture-grade front.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop
steps.body: Center the base upside down on the underside of the round top and predrill through the inner apron into the top with 2" screws — go slow and watch your depth so you don't drill through the top surface. Sand and finish the base and top together for a consistent look.

steps.title: Mount the bun feet
steps.body: Mount the surface-mount leg plates to the bottom of the base and thread the bun feet in. If your feet didn't come with hanger bolts, skip the plates and instead screw up through the bottom shelf into each foot with a long, predrilled screw so the foot doesn't split.

images.alt: Round dining table with two open storage shelves built into the base
